source: Invested by Aleph — Semiconductors, China/Taiwan fabs, global warming (China's Not Getting Taiwan's Fabs)
summary: Global warming is a solved problem due to solar and battery economics; the world will run on sunlight within one to two generations
summary_extended: Baker argues that global warming is effectively a solved problem driven by the relentless economics of solar and battery technology, where solar costs decline every year while all other energy sources become more expensive over time. He supports this view with the observation that US per-capita emissions have already returned to approximately 1920 levels, and notes that China's aggressive solar and EV buildout was motivated by national security imperatives — having no domestic fossil fuel reserves — rather than environmental ideology. Baker credits Elon Musk and Tesla specifically with doing more for decarbonization than all climate activists combined, and sees fusion energy as additional long-term optionality on top of the solar/battery transition. The investment implication is a structural long-term bullish view on solar and battery storage, with battery storage manufacturing capacity identified as the key remaining bottleneck to full transition; however, Baker names no specific equities in this segment, making direct ticker implications unambiguous.
detail: Baker argues solar costs decline every year while all other energy sources get more expensive. US is back to ~1920 per-capita emissions levels. China's solar/EV push was driven by national security (no domestic fossil fuels), not ideology. Fusion as additional upside optionality. Elon/Tesla did more for decarbonization than all activists combined. Battery storage manufacturing capacity is the key remaining bottleneck.
confidence_evidence: Baker states 'I genuinely believe global warming is a solved problem' and 'the world's going to run on sunlight' with economic reasoning
quote_fragment: I genuinely believe global warming is a solved problem
